sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a complaint of knee pain in the right knee, which started 5 days ago after a skiing accident on Lake Martin.
The patient reported that they twisted their knee when landing from a jump and felt a stretching sensation, followed by swelling.
The patient denied hearing or feeling anything pop during the incident.
The pain is exacerbated by squatting and bending.
The patient has not experienced similar injuries to this knee before.
The patient has a history of high blood pressure, which they manage with lisinopril 20mg daily.
Past medical history: The patient had their tonsils removed.
The patient denies any other significant medical history.
Review of systems: The patient denies any numbness or tingling in the foot.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable, with no lymphadenopathy.
The heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with no murmur.
The lung exam is clear to auscultation bilaterally.
The musculoskeletal exam of the right knee reveals ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion.
The patient experiences pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, negative varus and valgus test, negative Lachman sign, palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, and no lower extremity edema.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily for pain and inflammation management.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I advised the patient to rest their knee for a while to avoid exacerbating the injury.
I also ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid profile.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: I will contact the patient once the x-ray results are available to discuss further management.
